Title: Auxin-producing bacteria promote barley rhizosheath formation

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes barley root hair growth and rhizosheath formation, improving water and nutrient uptake and increasing grain yield under field conditions.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher transcriptional activity of Flavobacteriaceae and Paenibacillaceae MAGs in acid soil than alkaline soil under moderate soil drying.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: moderate soil drying; acid versus alkaline soil; NPA auxin transport inhibitor
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: metagenomics; metatranscriptomics; bacterial isolation and inoculation; mutant analysis; NPA treatment; field co-inoculation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: IAA-producing bacterial inoculation enhances barley rhizosheath formation and grain yield; inhibition of auxin transport reduces the rhizosheath-promoting effect.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Flavobacteriaceae and Paenibacillaceae enriched in acid-soil rhizosheath carry and express IAA-biosynthesis-related genes. 2. Isolated C. culicis and P. polymyxa produce IAA and promote barley root hair elongation and rhizosheath formation. 3. Their IAA-defective mutants lose this promotion, and co-inoculation increases field grain yield mainly through spike number.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper’s central nutrient-metabolism-relevant product is microbially produced IAA affecting plant architecture rather than a classical grain nutrient composition trait. No discrete non-enzyme common core gene was experimentally centered in the Results.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Develop microbial consortia based on IAA-producing C. culicis and P. polymyxa for enhancing rhizosheath formation, water/nutrient acquisition, and barley yield under soil drying.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: 16S rRNA amplicon sequencing: PRJNA867556; metagenome: PRJCA016632; metatranscriptome: PRJCA016646

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes root hair growth and rhizosheath formation, contributing to plant water and nutrient use efficiency and drought adaptation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: bacterial culture supernatant; acts in barley rhizosphere/rhizosheath
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: tryptophan-dependent IAA biosynthesis / tryptophan biosynthesis supporting IAA production
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: tryptophan supply branch feeding IAA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: indole-3-glycerol phosphate in tryptophan biosynthesis; indirectly supports IAA production
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of indole-3-glycerol phosphate in tryptophan biosynthesis, thereby supporting precursor availability for microbial IAA production.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: moderate soil drying; acid or alkaline soil
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene deletion mutant; bacterial IAA quantification; barley inoculation assay; root hair and rhizosheath phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion of trpC in C. culicis significantly reduces IAA production and abolishes promotion of barley root hair elongation and rhizosheath formation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. trpC was identified in the C. culicis genome as an IAA-biosynthesis-related gene. 2. Deletion of trpC significantly reduced IAA production in C. culicis. 3. The ΔtrpC mutant failed to significantly promote barley root hair length or rhizosheath formation compared with wild-type C. culicis under moderate soil drying.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: IAA production was reduced but not eliminated in the mutant, indicating additional IAA pathways may operate.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful microbial engineering target to maintain bacterial auxin production in inoculants that enhance barley rhizosheath formation and potentially grain yield under drying soils.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: bacterial whole-genome sequencing: C. culicis BioProject PRJCA016210
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper indicates the IPyA pathway is the main IAA metabolic pathway in C. culicis and P. polymyxa, but trpC was targeted in C. culicis because it is important for tryptophan biosynthesis; its effect on IAA is indirect via precursor supply.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes root hair growth and rhizosheath formation, contributing to plant water and nutrient use efficiency and drought adaptation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: bacterial culture supernatant; acts in barley rhizosphere/rhizosheath
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: indole-3-pyruvic acid (IPyA) pathway of IAA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates indole-3-pyruvate to indole-3-acetaldehyde in the IPyA pathway leading to IAA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: moderate soil drying; acid or alkaline soil
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene deletion mutant; bacterial IAA quantification; barley inoculation assay; root hair and rhizosheath phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion of ipdC in P. polymyxa significantly reduces IAA production and abolishes promotion of barley root hair elongation and rhizosheath formation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ipdC was identified in the P. polymyxa genome, and the IPyA pathway was described as the main IAA biosynthetic pathway. 2. Deletion of ipdC significantly reduced IAA production. 3. The ΔipdC mutant no longer significantly increased barley root hair length or rhizosheath formation, unlike wild-type P. polymyxa.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: IAA production was reduced but not completely abolished in the mutant, implying additional IAA synthesis pathways may exist.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for selecting or engineering microbial inoculants with robust IAA production to improve barley rhizosheath formation and yield in acid or alkaline soils.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: bacterial whole-genome sequencing: P. polymyxa BioProject PRJNA908138
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper explicitly states IPDC is a key enzyme necessary for IAA production in P. polymyxa.
